channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Newcastle United host West Ham United in key Premier League relegation match
item.description: West Ham United travel to St James' Park to face Newcastle United in a crucial Premier League fixture as both teams battle relegation. Currently, West Ham are inside the relegation zone by a single po...

item.title: Using Idle Land to Boost Food Security and Rural Economies in South Africa
item.description: Reginald Zalisile Mayekiso, founder of Olifantshoek Trading Enterprise (OTE), is championing a practical solution to South Africa’s growing food insecurity problem. He argues that the country possesse...

item.title: MFM Ajah Mega Axis Organises 2026 Music Concert to Celebrate God’s Faithfulness and Seek Divine Intervention
item.description: The Mountain of Fire and Miracles Ministries (MFM) Ajah Mega Axis don dey organise one big music concert wey go hold on Sunday, May 24, 2026. The programme wey dem theme am 'Sing Praises to God' go ta...

item.title: HORI7ON, KAIA, and 1st.One join forces for new music project 'Sulong'
item.description: Filipino boy group HORI7ON has joined forces with fellow P-pop artists KAIA and 1st.One for an exciting musical collaboration titled 'Sulong'. The collaboration aims to bring together their unique sty...

item.title: Berta Linson and Roger Mills: Pioneering Mississippi’s First Legal Interracial Marriage
item.description: In August 1970, Berta Linson, a 24-year-old Black student at Jackson State College, and Roger Mills, a 24-year-old white law clerk for the NAACP Legal Defense Fund, made history as Mississippi’s first...

item.title: Cape Town Marathon set to aim for Abbott World Marathon Majors status
item.description: The 2026 Sanlam Cape Town Marathon (CTM), scheduled for next Sunday, is under evaluation to potentially join the prestigious Abbott World Marathon Majors (AWMM), alongside global events such as London...

item.title: Taxi boss Joe Sibanyoni faces court over alleged R2.2m extortion
item.description: Madoda Johannes Sibanyoni, widely known as 'Joe Ferrari', has established a significant transport empire in South Africa despite growing up in poverty and surviving multiple assassination attempts. Kn...

item.title: A Retrospective Look at Liza Minnelli’s Early Career in the 1960s
item.description: Liza Minnelli, born on March 12, 1946, emerged as a dynamic force in American entertainment during the 1960s. The daughter of legendary actress Judy Garland, she quickly established her own identity a...

item.title: Philippine government asks Supreme Court to reject Dela Rosa petition against ICC arrest warrant
item.description: Hinimok ng pamahalaan ng Pilipinas ang Korte Suprema na ibasura ang petisyon ni Senador Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa na naglalayong harangin ang pagpapatupad ng arrest warrant mula sa International Crimina...

item.title: New Fossil Finds in Ethiopia Reveal Multiple Early Human Species Coexisting
item.description: Recent fossil discoveries in Ethiopia's Ledi Geraru region are reshaping our understanding of human evolution. Researchers found thirteen ancient teeth from both early Homo and a previously unidentifi...

item.title: Young Nairobi Mother Faces Loss of Husband Days After Childbirth
item.description: In a tragic turn of events in Nairobi, a young mother, Belinda, has been left devastated after her husband died just a few days following the birth of their newborn. The husband, in his early twenties...

item.title: Paul McCartney closes Season 51 of SNL with surprise encore performance
item.description: Paul McCartney, the legendary former Beatle, concluded the 51st season of Saturday Night Live with an unexpected encore performance. The 83-year-old musician returned to the show on 16th May 2026 to p...

item.title: Qantas flight diverts to Tahiti due to disruptive passenger incident
item.description: A Qantas long-haul flight from Melbourne to Dallas was forced to divert to Papeete, Tahiti, after a passenger allegedly became aggressive and bit a flight attendant mid-flight. The incident occurred o...

item.title: Constitutional Court reviews legality of Magudumana’s deportation and its effect on criminal prosecution
item.description: The Constitutional Court of South Africa is currently examining the legal ramifications of the deportation of celebrity doctor Nandipha Magudumana from Tanzania to South Africa. Magudumana, who faces ...

item.title: How World Bank plans to improve its complaints and accountability systems
item.description: Since 1994, the World Bank don dey run the Inspection Panel, di first independent body wey dey check complaints from communities wey suffer because projects no follow proper procedures. Till now, di p...

item.title: Notable Clubs That Stayed Unbeaten Yet Missed Out on League Titles
item.description: For football, to finish a season without losing any match dey usually enough to make club win league title. But e get some rare cases wey show say even unbeaten team fit still miss out. One big exampl...

item.title: Iconic 1960s Women’s Fashion Through Vintage Photography
item.description: The 1960s was a groundbreaking decade in fashion, marked by a shift from the structured elegance of the 1950s to expressive, youthful styles. Early in the decade, the 'Ladylike' look was popular, with...

item.title: Filipino Teacher Receives MBE Honor in the United Kingdom
item.description: A Filipino educator has been recognized for outstanding contributions to education and community service by being awarded the prestigious Most Excellent Order of the British Empire (MBE) in the United...

item.title: DJ Shannyn Hill Becomes Official DJ for Toronto’s New WNBA Team
item.description: Shannyn Hill, 29, has emerged as the official DJ for Toronto’s newly established WNBA franchise, the Tempo. Raised in Mississauga within a traditional Jamaican family, Hill faced cultural and familial...

item.title: IMD introduces percentile-based heatwave alerts to better capture extreme conditions
item.description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) is overhauling its heatwave alert system, introducing a percentile-based approach for the first time. Under the new system, a heat alert will be issued when m...
